---
title: "Create files for checking the reliability of contributorship coding"
author: "Anonymized for peer review (NC)"
reviewer: "Anonymized for peer review (JT)"
output: word_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

This code creates a spreadsheet for checking the reliability of contributorship coding.

```{r}
# load libaries
library('tidyverse')
library('here')

# set seed
set.seed(1967)

# specify directory
i_am('btpsych24a_cont_reliability_prep.Rmd')

# open and process data
DF.c <-
  # open data
  read.csv(here('data',
                'contributorship',
                'btpsych24a_cont_coding.csv'),
           na.string = "N/A") %>% 
  
  # filter
  filter(
    #remove instances without contributorship statements
    !is.na(contributorship.statement),
    contributorship.statement != "",
    
    # remove instances where coders recommended removal
    remove.revise != 1,
    
    # remove instances where there wasn't anything to code
    !(is.na(conception) &
        is.na(execution) &
        is.na(analysis) &
        is.na(draft.writing) &
        is.na(review.writing) &
        is.na(funding)
      ),
    
    # remove instances where G coded
    Coder == "J"
    ) %>% 
  
  # randomly select 20 cases
  slice_sample(n = 20) %>% 
  
  # clear data
  mutate(contributorship.statement = "",
         conception = "",
         execution = "",
         analysis = "",
         draft.writing = "",
         review.writing = "",
         funding = "",
         Coder = "G")
```

Export
```{r}
# if file doesn't exist (it should), create it
if (!file.exists(here('data',
                      'contributorship',
                      'btpsych24a_cont_coding_rel.csv')
                 )
    ){
  write.csv(DF.c,
          here('data',
               'contributorship',
               'btpsych24a_cont_coding_rel.csv'),
          row.names = F)
}
```
